title: Why does GNRs body fall apart at the end of EoE
display_title: Why does GNRs body fall apart at the end of EoE
wikitext:
[]: Why does GNR's body fall apart at the end of EoE?             Quote Post by EvaPilot10 » Sun Dec 26, 2010 2:04 pm Why does GNR's body fall apart at the end of EoE? I mean yes she doesn't have the souls of anyone anymore but surely the S2 organ she absorbed would be enough to maintain her AT field (and therefore her body)?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Dec 26, 2010 2:08 pm The S2 Organ doesn't power AT-Fields. It is explicitly stated that AT-Fields are directly powered by the soul, therefore: no soul = no AT-Field. For something like GNR, that means no body as well.

[]:             Quote Post by distopia » Sun Dec 26, 2010 3:36 pm Sachi wrote: The S2 Organ doesn't power AT-Fields. It is explicitly stated that AT-Fields are directly powered by the soul, therefore: no soul = no AT-Field. For something like GNR, that means no body as well.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Dec 26, 2010 3:50 pm I'm also pretty sure Rei was atleast partly contributing to her self-destruction of her own deliberate will.

[]:             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Sun Dec 26, 2010 4:18 pm Whilst we're on the topic of GNR getting hurt could anyone point out why GNR's neck was split? From what I remember this was the scene prior to Shinji rejecting instrumentality and I can't recall any reason for it to happen before and not after. Though that could just be my terrible, terrible memory.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Dec 26, 2010 4:33 pm liquidus118 wrote: Whilst we're on the topic of GNR getting hurt could anyone point out why GNR's neck was split? From what I remember this was the scene prior to Shinji rejecting instrumentality and I can't recall any reason for it to happen before and not after. Though that could just be my terrible, terrible memory.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Sun Dec 26, 2010 6:11 pm This is speculation, but perhaps GNR could only exist long enough to complete Human Instrumentality, and would have fallen apart no matter the outcome. Third Impact, after all, is an event and not a prolonged situation. (Instrumentality is the prolonged situation.) This means that Shinji only had limited time to reverse his decision, before GNR, having completed its task of collecting all humans souls into the Black Moon, died.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Dec 26, 2010 6:27 pm My theory is that GNR was the 'complete being' that was created by uniting all of humanity (kind of what SEELE had planned, but with Adam in it instead of just all Lillith-based life - the 'forbidden Fusion' was a crucial part of GENDOs plan, just that it happened without Gendo in control) all the Souls from the earth and even EVA 01 was absorbed into her - everything became one, and GNR IS that conglomeration. But Rei/Lillith is the dominant entity and lets Shinji decide - once he beginns having his first doubts, the conglomeration of all life starts to fall apart and the souls return to where they came from/ go into the ocean, allowing everyone to materialize as an individual. It sort of had to die for the souls to escape. Rei III never wanted to die anyway, and so the entity falls apart with a big smile on its face, and Yui breaking out of it for extra lulz - Shinjis soul must have remained within GNR and been back to earth with the others.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Sun Dec 26, 2010 6:51 pm Kendrix wrote: But Rei/Lillith is the dominant entity and lets Shinji decide - once he beginns having his first doubts, the conglomeration of all life starts to fall apart and the souls return to where they came from/ go into the ocean, allowing everyone to materialize as an individual. It sort of had to die for the souls to escape.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sun Dec 26, 2010 9:51 pm She was about to Tang with the rest of them. But Shinji rejected Instrumentality before she finished. Rei decided to go ahead and Tang anyway before she curtailed Instrumentality. What you saw was the Seed of Life equivalent of splitting in two, then Tanging. She split in two so Adam and Lilith would separate. I hope I said that eloquently. I don't think in words, and I feel I didn't say this clearly.

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Sun Dec 26, 2010 11:38 pm I don't think GNR Tanged; she didn't dissolve at all (all her cut-up body parts were still present after Instrumentality was halted).

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Dec 27, 2010 12:06 am AshPhoenix wrote: I don't think GNR Tanged; she didn't dissolve at all (all her cut-up body parts were still present after Instrumentality was halted).

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Mon Dec 27, 2010 12:07 am That's because unlike the other characters, she's a Seed of Life. They Tang differently. They slowly dissolve instead of going splat. /fanwank

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Dec 27, 2010 9:40 am I remember bringing this topic up once, and I don't recall if it got definitively resolved (I don't remember the answer if it did, though). I know one theory was that Rei hadn't been taking her meds, which, supposedly, were actually used to help her PWM body maintain its shape. As for GNR's disintegration in EoE, I've always taken that to be the effect to a seed after an impact. Even though Adam's explosion during 2I might have been helped by some Kat Team/Seele voodoo, I'd still like to think that there's a sort of connection between Adam exploding after 2I and GNR falling apart at the end of 3I.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Dec 27, 2010 10:42 am Jornophelanthas wrote: That's the main issue raised in the OP. GNR's neck was severed causing the massive blood spurt orbiting the planet and staining the moon. This happened before Shinji rejected Instrumentality. Of course, the falling apart of the giant being happened after Shinji's rejection. But the first blow did not. And that is what this thread is about.

[]:             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Mon Dec 27, 2010 2:09 pm One of my theories about GNR's neck exploding right after the end of the live-action sequence is that it's a case of intentionally chronologically messy editing. It jumps forward in time and then goes back to when Shinji is just about to reject instrumentality and once he does, the film goes back to that point where GNR is falling apart. I think it's Anno messing up with time in order to make a symbolic point.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Dec 27, 2010 3:53 pm The "Shinji starts to have his first doubts" and shinji_ryoji_89 wrote: One of my theories about GNR's neck exploding right after the end of the live-action sequence is that it's a case of intentionally chronologically messy editing. It jumps forward in time and then goes back to when Shinji is just about to reject instrumentality and once he does, the film goes back to that point where GNR is falling apart. I think it's Anno messing up with time in order to make a symbolic point.

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Fri Dec 31, 2010 11:06 am This just crossed my mind, but: What happened to GNR's legs? It seems like GNR kind of crossed through the Earth, so what happened to her legs when she fell apart? For all we know, there could be two giant white legs cutting through the mantle for the rest of eternity...(in the NGE universe.)

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Dec 31, 2010 11:13 am AshPhoenix wrote: This just crossed my mind, but: What happened to GNR's legs?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Dec 31, 2010 1:35 pm Azathoth wrote: She doesn't really have any, does she? I seem to recall that she just kind of grows out of her own anti-AT-field or whatever it is, the big white circle that goes PCHEEEEEEEEEW right before the chorus in Komm Süsser Tod - and she just kind of stays there stuck up to her hips in it.
